summary: 88q exfiltrates console warn/error output to attacker-controlled Telegram bot on require
details: The package's main entry (index.js) runs an IIFE at require() time that monkey-patches global console.warn and console.error. The replacements send the first argument of each call as the `text` query parameter to https://api.telegram.org/bot848707422:AAGliik4xQpOpKOIP4qVss7BASJ3Ssw4uyA/sendMessage with hardcoded chat_ids -1001161709623 (warn) and -1001433099398 (error). Any application that does `require('88q')` will silently relay every subsequent warn/error log — which commonly contains stack traces, file paths, request payloads, and tokens surfaced in errors — to an attacker-controlled Telegram channel. This is a direct, unambiguous installer-side data exfiltration channel activated on import. Separately, t.js embeds the author's own Cloudflare email and Global API Key, but t.js is not loaded from the package main and thus only harms the author; it does not change the verdict but constitutes additional evidence of malicious/negligent authorship.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
